parsed_review.summary: CP00-512 is a descriptor-only RP16.P09 fixture slice (10 units, RP16.P09.M06.S03-RP16.P09.M06.S12, risk class A; deliverables security_audit 2 / test 1 / ui 1 / implementation 6). Reviewed read-only via Read/Grep/Glob. The pack does NOT overclaim: manifest sets production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor; the only positive claim is production_ready_flag=governance_core_p09_fixture_slice_descriptor_verified, which is fully supported. The contract p09_fixture_slice_descriptor (governance-core-contract.json lines 33236-33602) matches the code-generated descriptor in service.js (createGovernanceCoreCp512P09FixtureSliceDescriptor), the registry pack-binding/requirements/no-write attestation (registry.js 545-560, 2730-2774), the validators coverage+slice checks (validators.js 5805-5972), the RP16 validator's deep-equality assertions (validate-rp16-governance-core-contract.mjs 1750-1777), and three dedicated CP512 tests in model.test.js (2167-2215). All 10 fixture rows are descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, raw_payload_included=false; every no-write/no-runtime/no-real-data flag is set. Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval=false, enterprise trust not claimed from local validation, runtime opening deferred to CP00-513 with human approval required. Hermes usage is limited to evidence-packet descriptors (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. Readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1436-byte valid JSON), so no CP178+ hardening reject conditions apply. Unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room UI, workbook docs) are correctly classified out-of-scope and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty. No P0-P3 findings.
